bibliographic_data.title: Adversary for Social Good: Leveraging Adversarial Attacks to Protect Personal Attribute Privacy
bibliographic_data.abstract: Social media has drastically reshaped the world that allows billions of people to engage in such interactive environments to conveniently create and share content with the public. Among them, text data (e.g., tweets, blogs) maintains the basic yet important social activities and generates a rich source of user-oriented information. While those explicit sensitive user data like credentials has been significantly protected by all means, personal private attribute (e.g., age, gender, location) disclosure due to inference attacks is somehow challenging to avoid, especially when powerful natural language processing (NLP) techniques have been effectively deployed to automate attribute inferences from implicit text data. This puts users’ attribute privacy at risk. To address this challenge, in this paper, we leverage the inherent vulnerability of machine learning to adversarial attacks, and design a novel text-space Adversarial attack for Social Good, called Adv4SG. In other words, we cast the problem of protecting personal attribute privacy as an adversarial attack formulation problem over the social media text data to defend against NLP-based attribute inference attacks.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper introduces Adv4SG, a novel Adversarial Attack for Social Good that leverages machine learning vulnerabilities to protect personal attribute privacy in social media text data.
core_content.key_methodology: Adv4SG proceeds with a sequence of word perturbations under given constraints such that the probed attribute cannot be identified correctly. It considers social media properties and introduces cost-effective mechanisms to expedite attribute obfuscation over text data under the black-box setting.
outcomes: Effectively degrades inference accuracy with less computational cost
Substantially helps mitigate the impacts of inference attacks
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this paper, we address the challenge of protecting personal attribute privacy in social media by leveraging machine learning vulnerabilities to adversarial attacks. Specifically, we introduce Adv4SG, a novel Adversarial Attack for Social Good that operates on text data from social media platforms like tweets and blogs. The goal is to protect user attributes such as age, gender, location, etc., which can be inferred using NLP techniques. Adv4SG works by perturbing the text in a way that makes it difficult for inference attacks to correctly identify the probed attribute. This method considers social media properties and uses cost-effective mechanisms to obfuscate text data under a black-box setting, thereby reducing computational costs while maintaining effectiveness.
